title: lycopodium struthioloides var. margaritae
intro: Lycopodium struthioloides var. margaritae, also known as the spotted club moss, is a type of lycopodium species that belongs to the lycopodiophyta class and is native to certain regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lycopodium struthioloides var. margaritae include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Lycopodium struthioloides var. margaritae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
